nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Berkeley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the numerous places so named, such as Berkeley in Gloucestershire, named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Compare Scottish Barclay .
Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of   Berkowitz  .

variants.name: Barclay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name of English origin, from any of several places so called, such as Berkley in Somerset or Berkeley in Gloucestershire, both named in Old English with be(o)rc ‘birch’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

History: The surname was brought to Scotland from southwestern England in the 12th century; Walter de Berchelai or Berkelai was Chamberlain of Scotland in 1165. It was long assumed that they were from the family that held Berkeley Castle in Gloucestershire, but the historian G. W. S. Barrow (1973, The Kingdom of the Scots) adduced evidence that they are more probably from Berkley in Somerset.

variants.name: Berkley
meanings: English: variant of   Berkeley  . Compare Scottish   Barclay  .

nameMeanings.name: Laird
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: status name for a landlord or landowner, from northern Middle English laverd ‘lord’.

variants.name: Leard
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Laird  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Towie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Town
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Kent): topographic name from Middle English toun(e), t(h)one, tun(e) ‘town, village, settlement’ (Old English tūn), often in the senses ‘primary settlement within an area’, ‘manor, estate’, and ‘hamlet, farm’, for someone who lived in such a place.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: Lady Hugh de Berkeley Laird of Towie was born about 1230, in Berkeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Hugh de Berkeley 6th Laird of Towie.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Hugh de Berkeley Laird of Towie est née environ 1230, à Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Hugh de Berkeley 6th Laird of Towie.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Hugh de Berkeley Laird of Towie è nata il circa 1230 nel Berkeley, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Hugh de Berkeley 6th Laird of Towie.
